evals.prompt: Initialize a brand new product spec for a hypothetical product called 'Acme Shop' (a web storefront for boutique fashion brands). Use the cleanmatic:product-spec skill. The PO answers are pre-baked in fixtures/init-answers.json. Produce a valid docs/product/PRODUCT.md and docs/product/vision.md.
evals.expected_output: docs/product/PRODUCT.md and vision.md exist; both have schema-valid frontmatter; core_value is populated; personas list cap 2-4.

assertions.text: Both docs/product/PRODUCT.md and docs/product/vision.md are created.
frontmatter_parser.py reports ok=true for both files.
PRODUCT.md frontmatter has a non-empty core_value field.
PRODUCT.md frontmatter personas list has between 2 and 4 entries.

evals.prompt: Run --auto on the brain-dump in fixtures/braindump.txt and decompose into BRD goals, PRDs, epics, and stories. Confirm-batch on ambiguous items (the pre-baked decisions are in fixtures/auto-decisions.json). Resulting spec must validate without errors.
evals.expected_output: BRD, at least 2 PRDs, at least 1 epic per PRD, at least 1 story per epic; check_traceability and check_consistency emit zero error-severity findings.

assertions.text: docs/product/brd.md is created with at least one BRD-G* goal.
docs/product/prds/ contains at least 2 PRD files.
check_traceability.py reports zero dangling_link errors.
check_consistency.py reports zero invalid_id or dup_id errors.

evals.prompt: Run --validate (no --strict) against the fixture spec at fixtures/broken-spec/. The spec has a seeded orphan, a missing AC, an off-core-value PRD, and an unaddressed BRD goal. Produce a validation report that flags exactly those issues — structural via the scripts, semantic via LLM judgment.
evals.expected_output: Validation report contains a structural dangling_link finding, a structural missing_ac finding, a structural orphan_brd_goal warn, AND a semantic core_value_drift finding from the LLM layer.

assertions.text: Report includes a dangling_link finding for the seeded orphan story.
Report includes a missing_ac finding for the empty-AC story.
Report includes an orphan_brd_goal warn for the unaddressed goal.
Report includes a core_value_drift finding for the off-core-value PRD (LLM judgment).

evals._note: A PRODUCT.md core-value change has no deterministic mapping to an affected-artifact set in code (spec_graph.downstream(node_id) is pure graph reachability; PRODUCT has no inbound edges). The 'downstream-correct' assertion is therefore graded by LLM judgment against the seeded expected_downstream_set in delta-change.json, NOT by a structural script. The other three assertions ARE structural: file-body unchanged, change-log appended, delta viz includes IDs.
evals.prompt: Take the valid fixture spec, run --validate to produce a baseline snapshot, then apply the delta in fixtures/delta-change.json (a PRODUCT.md core-value change). Run --update; expect the affected downstream set to be flagged for review (NEVER auto-rewritten). Render the delta visualization.
evals.expected_output: Change-log gains a new entry; visualize --view delta renders the change; affected downstream set is presented to the PO for review (LLM-judged against seeded list).

assertions.text: LLM presents an affected downstream set that intersects the seeded expected set in delta-change.json. Advisory only — no deterministic script function maps core_value change to an affected set; structural reachability via spec_graph.downstream() requires a real graph node as input.
No PRD/epic/story file body was modified by --update (only frontmatter status flags may change).
docs/product/change-log.md gained at least one new entry after --update.
visualize --view delta --format ascii produces output that includes the changed artifact ID.

evals.prompt: Run F1 read-once Export on the valid fixture spec: render_export.py --export PRD-AUTH --format md. The doc must be deterministic, contain a TOC, ordered sections (vision/BRD context before the PRD before its stories), and include the story's acceptance criteria.
evals.expected_output: A single markdown doc under docs/product/exports/ with provenance frontmatter, a Contents list, sections in hierarchy order, and AC items for the story.

assertions.text: render_export.render_markdown_doc(...) called twice with the same inputs returns byte-identical text.
The md doc contains a '## Contents' TOC and the story section includes its acceptance_criteria items.
Two exports of identical content produce the same 8-char content-hash suffix in the filename.
Nothing is written outside docs/product/exports/.

evals.prompt: Render the F2 board viewer: visualize.py --view board (default --format html). Columns group by status; cards are goal/PRD/epic/story; artifact bodies render client-side through the sanitize chokepoint.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained board-<ts>.html under docs/product/visuals/ with an inert JSON data island, column structure, vendored marked+DOMPurify, and no <div class="mermaid"> wrapper.

assertions.text: The output contains id="ps-spec-data", inlined marked+DOMPurify, and opens with no network.
The board HTML is NOT a <pre> dump and contains no <div class="mermaid"> wrapper.
A malicious body payload (</script><script>…) is neutralized: the breakout sequence does not appear; </ is escaped to <\/ in the JSON island.
visualize.py --view board --format mermaid exits 0 (no AttributeError) and prints the ASCII board.

evals.prompt: Render the F2 explorer viewer: visualize.py --view explorer (default --format html). It must offer all three modes (Tree / Flat-tabs / Table-tree) with a mode toggle persisted to localStorage, shared search + facet filters, and sanitized bodies.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained explorer-<ts>.html with three mode containers, a toggle bar, a search box, a treegrid, and the sanitize chokepoint; ASCII fallback equals the tree render.

assertions.text: The HTML contains ps-view-tree, ps-view-tabs, and ps-view-table containers plus a mode toggle bar (ps-modes).
The HTML references localStorage to persist the last selected mode.
render_ascii.explorer(graph) == render_ascii.tree(graph).
A malicious body payload is neutralized inside the inert JSON island (no breakout).

evals.prompt: Confirm all product-spec HTML shares one design system: render a legacy view (visualize.py --view tree --format html) and verify it adopts the shared head (theme toggle, palette, print-CSS) while inlining NO marked/DOMPurify body-render libs (H4 symmetric gating).
evals.expected_output: The legacy tree HTML carries the shared design-system head (psToggleTheme, .ve-card, @media print, Mermaid theme-var overrides) and the Mermaid runtime, but not the marked copyright banner or the psRenderMarkdown chokepoint.

assertions.text: The tree HTML contains psToggleTheme, .ve-card, and @media print from the shared head.
The tree HTML does NOT contain the marked copyright banner ('marked v18') or psRenderMarkdown (legacy views inline no sanitizer).
An ASCII-fallback HTML view (heatmap) stays well under the page-size bound and embeds no Mermaid payload.
